other_observers: Chris Wood; Chris Wood found and identified the bird; Joe and Steve Mammoser saw it a few days later
specific_location: one block in from Hwy 144 in center of Weldona
description: A medium-sized hummer, Chris first noticed the bird coming to a Trumpet Creeper hedge a block north of Hwy 144 and called out "Ruby-throated Hummer!" The bird was, apparently, a female, as it lacked any red gorget feathers. The bird had a longish, straight black bill and dark eyes. The crown, nape, back, and rump were all bright green. There was a relatively distinct dark auricular patch below and behind the small white post-ocular spot and separating the green of the crown from the white of the throat. The throat and chest were bright white and the sides and flanks were splotchy greenish and white creating the appearance of being washed with grayish-green. The undertail coverts were pale and unmarked, as far as I could tell.  The tail was green (basally) and black, with obvious white tips to the outer 2 or 3 pairs of rectrices (I couldn't be sure whether 2 or 3 in the brief look I had at the spread tail). The tail extended more than a bit beyond the wingtips when the bird was perched. The bird's "shoulder" (lesser coverts) were the same bright green as the back and the remiges were blackish. Through the scope, we could easily see that P10 was narrow and pointed, rather than wide and more rounded as in Black-chinned.Behaviors: hovering in front of Trumpet Creeper flowers and perched very low (~3' off ground) in a small treeCall: none heardPlumage: Basic
